summary: Discovery prose-coverage gaps: 4 assumptions (A1, A3, A4, A9), 7 ledger-OOS items (indices 1,2,3,4,7,8,9), 19 per-section OOS items (across S1-S6), and TD chosen_verb are uncovered by the covers-graph.

uncovered_assumptions: ledger-v1.json#/assumption_ledger/2 (A3: local Apple Music data source)

context: Several uncovered claims are entailed by what IS covered: A1 (SDK shape) is verified by the entire MCA.TDIPA cluster; A3 (local Apple Music) by MCA.TDIPD.*; A4 (macOS) by MCA.TDIPA.2; A9 (no telemetry) by DCA.OOS.TELEMETRY.1. chosen_verb='display' is verified by PNV.1 which references it. The gap is in explicit covers-pointer wiring, not in behavioral verification. Section-level OOS items are mostly handoff demarcations (e.g., 'WebSocket transport (S3)') that are implicitly verified by depends_on edges and per-section ownership.
recommendation: Defer to Arbiter for a Principle-C strict-pass decision. The simplest fix is to extend covers fields on existing assertions to also point at the related uncovered fields (multi-target covers, or duplicate covers on existing assertions). No new behavioral assertions are needed — only the schema-graph link.
